title: Update working memory from issue 351 Docker sandbox docs session
summary: Durable context from session 41cda726 about Docker socket documentation, restricted-host sandbox fallback, and the separate browser sandbox follow-up.

claims.text: Sandbox backend selection keeps WASM and NoSandbox out of auto detection: create_sandbox returns NoSandbox only when SandboxMode::Off is configured, select_backend handles wasm explicitly, and auto_detect_backend prefers Apple Container on macOS, then Podman, Docker, and finally restricted-host when Docker is unavailable or no container runtime is available.

The Docker deployment docs now say the Docker socket mount is recommended for full container isolation, but without the socket Moltis automatically falls back to the restricted-host sandbox, runs commands inside the Moltis container, and lacks filesystem and network isolation.

Issue 351 deliberately rejected documenting the socketless Docker case as either shell tools failing or Moltis running in "no sandbox" mode; future Docker docs should describe that automatic case as restricted-host fallback instead.

Follow-up from issue 351: investigate browser sandbox behavior separately because the issue discussion called out that browser tooling does not auto-fallback the same way shell command sandboxing does; the Docker docs fix did not implement that behavior change.

sources.title: SWE-chat Claude Code session 41cda726: issue 351 Docker sandbox docs fix

metadata.reason: The session analyzed issue 351, identified the old Docker docs claims that shell commands fail or run with no sandbox as wrong, and the user approved replacing them with restricted-host fallback language.
The issue analysis recorded browser sandbox fallback inconsistency as a separate point, while the implemented and accepted change only corrected Docker documentation.
